classification_rationale: A standalone Python package (pyproject.toml:6 name="afi-benchkit", entrypoint afi-bench=afi_benchkit.cli:main) for deterministic validator benchmarking. README.md:22-26 explicitly bounds it: "This is a benchmark and verification toolkit only... Does NOT contain DAG/engine/scoring runtime logic... Does NOT contain smart contracts or on-chain code." It defines no protocol schemas, no contracts, no canonical type defs, and is not part of the reference ingest->DAG->vault->mint spine. It produces benchmark metrics (PoI capability, PoInsight usefulness) and a benchmark-local validator "reputation" score that influences selection/allocation, not protocol finality. It is a supporting/verification tool, not NORMATIVE (no spec/schema authority) and not the protocol REFERENCE_IMPL spine.
primary_purpose: A standalone, container-packaged Python toolkit that runs deterministic, seeded validator benchmarks (PoI capability and PoInsight usefulness suites) over CSV datasets, emitting hash-stamped canonical artifacts, metrics, calibration/capability plots, and a composite "reputation" score with confidence intervals for reproducible validator verification.
protocol_touchpoints: replay/determinism (hash-locked reproducible benchmark outputs, golden-image verification)
registries/reputation (computes benchmark-derived validator reputation scores influencing selection/allocation)
scoring/metrics (PoI/PoInsight benchmark metrics: IC, hit-rate, Sharpe, coverage, vitality, latency)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: docker run ... ghcr.io/AFI-Protocol/afi-benchkit:stable afi-bench run --suite poinsight ... --seed 1337 ... --emit-scores  (presents a specific org-hosted container image as the canonical run vehicle)
source "$REPO_ROOT/../afi-cli-shared/scripts/lib/afi-shared.sh"  (hard sibling-path dependency on afi-cli-shared org tooling for benchmark generation)
Tier-3 verification - Hash-locked dependencies, bit-for-bit outputs  (a specific determinism/packaging tier is implied as the required verification mode)

contradictions.title: Benchmark 'reputation' score risks being read as protocol-canonical scoring
contradictions.detail: reputation.py computes a composite validator reputation (alpha*PoI + beta*PoInsight) with CIs and an env-tunable composite. The North Star says reputation/registries influence SELECTION & ALLOCATION only and MUST NOT override deterministic scoring or protocol finality. The repo's README does scope it as 'benchmark and verification toolkit only', so this is a presentation/clarity risk rather than a production violation, but the term 'reputation' plus tunable AFI_BENCHKIT_ALPHA/BETA could be mistaken for protocol-level reputation canon.
contradictions.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/reputation.py:65 "reputation_score = alpha * poi_score + beta * poinsight_score"; README.md:112 "Composite Reputation: Weighted combination (alpha=0.3 PoI + beta=0.7 PoInsight)"

contradictions.title: Hardcoded heuristic N=10 and assumed determinism=1.0/uptime=1.0 weaken claimed determinism rigor
contradictions.detail: The reputation bridge fabricates an effective sample size of 10 for PoInsight when canonical CSV is absent, and assumes determinism=1.0 and uptime=1.0 in the PoI raw score (weights are 0 so they do not affect output, but the comment 'Assume deterministic for now' indicates a placeholder). This undercuts the 'deterministic / evidence-weighted' framing for downstream consumers, though it is internal to a benchmark tool, not a protocol scoring path.
contradictions.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/reputation.py:119-120 "Could not determine exact sample size for PoInsight, using estimated N=10"; reputation.py:150 "determinism = 1.0  # Assume deterministic for now"

notable_findings.title: Self-declared boundaries: not protocol runtime, contracts, or DAG
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:22-26 "This is a benchmark and verification toolkit only - Does NOT contain DAG/engine/scoring runtime logic - Does NOT contain smart contracts or on-chain code - Does NOT contain UI components"
notable_findings.recommendation: Accept as SUPPORTING; no protocol-law claims. Keep these boundaries visible in any cross-repo map so it is not mistaken for the scoring DAG or token layer.

notable_findings.title: Determinism is real but local: re-run SHA256 equality + golden-image hashes + fixed timestamp for golden tests
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/tasks/poi01.py:123 "determinism_pass = hash_original == hash_rerun"; src/afi_benchkit/stamp.py:37-38 "if os.environ.get(\"AFI_BENCHKIT_GOLDEN_TEST\") == \"1\": utc_ts = \"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\""; README.md:13 "Tier-3 verification - Hash-locked dependencies, bit-for-bit outputs"
notable_findings.recommendation: This determinism model (pinned deps via requirements.lock.txt + bit-for-bit golden outputs) is a good candidate pattern to reference in VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC, but it is benchmark-scoped and does NOT implement the protocol's signal replay (no signalId/epoch/vault/TSSD). Tag as partial replay relevance only.

notable_findings.title: Provenance Stamp captures data_hash + git_sha + utc_ts but NOT protocol linkage fields
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/stamp.py:14-19 frozen dataclass Stamp(data_hash, cfg_hash, git_sha, utc_ts); footer format poi01.py:211 "data={short_hash} sha={git_sha} ts={stamp.utc_ts}"
notable_findings.recommendation: Stamp is a benchmark provenance record; it has no signalId/epoch/content-hash anchoring to on-chain commitment. No action; note it is not a replay-critical protocol artifact.

notable_findings.title: No mint/receipt/emissions/vault/TSSD/USS/CPJ touchpoints present
notable_findings.evidence: grep across .py/.md/.toml/.sh/.yml/.json for mongodb|vault|TSSD, usignal|USS|cpj|CPJ, mintForSignal|coordinateMint|beneficiary, AFIMintCoordinator|AFISignalReceipt|EmissionsMinted, kafka|postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb|snowflake -> ZERO hits
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirms this repo touches none of the commitment/evidence/ingest planes; it is orthogonal benchmark tooling. No portable-protocol normative obligations apply.

notable_findings.title: Composite reputation weights are env-tunable (alpha/beta/N0) and recomputed on merge
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/reputation.py:258-263 get_env_overrides() reads AFI_BENCHKIT_ALPHA/BETA/N0; reputation.py:318-324 merge recomputes "reputation_score = alpha * poi_score + beta * poinsight_score"
notable_findings.recommendation: Clarify in README that this 'reputation' is a benchmark-local validator score (selection/allocation input), explicitly NOT protocol reputation canon and NOT authoritative over deterministic scoring or finality, to avoid future conflation with REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION spec.

notable_findings.title: Hardcoded N=10 heuristic and placeholder determinism/uptime in reputation bridge
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_benchkit/reputation.py:117-120 "For the sample dataset, we know it has 10 rows after cleaning ... using estimated N=10"; reputation.py:150-153 "determinism = 1.0  # Assume deterministic for now ... uptime = 1.0"
notable_findings.recommendation: Fix-in-follow-up: derive N from actual report/CSV in all paths and remove sample-specific magic constant; either implement or remove the unused determinism/uptime terms to avoid misleading evidence-weighting claims.

notable_findings.title: Stale/relative cross-repo source path to afi-cli-shared
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag: benchmark generation/lock scripts assume a sibling-checkout layout of afi-cli-shared; document this hard dependency or vendor the shared lib so the public toolkit is runnable standalone.

notable_findings.title: Reference-suite artifacts mostly untracked; only METADATA.txt committed and references a non-HEAD git SHA
notable_findings.evidence: git ls-files examples/ -> only examples/reference-suite/METADATA.txt; METADATA.txt:3 "Git SHA: 9eb3a14" (HEAD is e3bb1b8); scores.json/plots/metrics are generated, not committed
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale: the committed METADATA.txt records a stale generation SHA and the canonical reference-suite outputs it advertises (README.md:51-58) are not in the repo, so the 'canonical reference artifacts' cannot be byte-verified from the checkout; regenerate and either commit or clearly mark as build-time only.

notable_findings.title: Tier-3 strict build enforces hash-locked deps (genuine determinism guarantee)
notable_findings.evidence: Dockerfile:27-28 STRICT=1 -> "pip download --require-hashes -r requirements.lock.txt"; Dockerfile:47-48 "pip install --no-index --find-links=/wheelhouse --require-hashes -r requirements.lock.txt"; Dockerfile:15 PYTHONHASHSEED=0
notable_findings.recommendation: Solid hermetic-build pattern; worth citing as a reference for how reference-impl repos should pin transform/dependency versions for replay, even though this repo is not itself replay-critical to the protocol.
